headline: The reported 'UAR not found in PATH' bug is a MISSING CAPABILITY, not a misconfiguration. LibreFang never installs, spawns, or supervises a UAR process anywhere: no UAR binary in the image (Dockerfile:177-178 links UAR in-process via --features uar-driver and copies only /usr/local/bin/librefang), no sidecar container (k8s/base/bossfang-deployment.yaml:64-68 has one container), and no spawn code. The error originates from the GENERIC channel sidecar supervisor (sidecar.rs:783 Command::new -> sidecar.rs:822-827 wrapper) when UAR is hand-configured as a [[sidecar_channels]] entry; resolve_sidecar_command (sidecar.rs:728) only covers the Telegram stem, so UAR falls through to a bare PATH lookup for a binary that was never shipped.
recommendation: SUPERSEDED BY D-1 (see decisions). Adopt UAR-as-sidecar and retire the in-process uar-driver link. The embedded model cannot express the goal (an in-process library has no process for a console to run/stop/test). Shape chosen at analyze: CHILD PROCESS IN-CONTAINER — librefang spawns UAR's `uar-sidecar` binary (READY:{port} stdout handshake, stdin-EOF shutdown), binary baked into the BossFang image via COPY --from=<uar-image>, resolved via current_exe() -> ~/.librefang/bin -> PATH so PATH is never load-bearing. The k8s native-sidecar-container shape was considered and REJECTED (a container cannot start/stop a sibling container from inside the pod, so it delivers use+test but not run).
correction_found: CLAUDE.md documents uar-driver as 'off by default, opt-in'. This is FALSE. librefang-kernel/Cargo.toml:16 requests features = ["uar-driver"] unconditionally, and librefang-cli -> librefang-kernel is not feature-gated, so EVERY shipped binary compiles UAR in. This is why the surrealdb =3.2.0 vs =3.2.1 exact-pin mismatch found during the 2026-07-11 upstream merge would have broken every build, not just an opt-in one.
blockers: G-1 no UAR binary shipped (blocked on upstream: UAR must build/publish `uar-sidecar`)
G-3 no spawn/supervision code (READY handshake + stdin-EOF shutdown on top of the existing supervisor)
G-4 no bundled-binary resolution for UAR (generalize resolve_sidecar_command, sidecar.rs:728)

decisions.D-1: Integration shape = child process in-container (user-confirmed). librefang spawns UAR's `uar-sidecar` binary as a supervised child; binary baked into the BossFang image via COPY --from=<uar-image> and resolved via current_exe() -> ~/.librefang/bin -> PATH, so PATH is never load-bearing. Rejected: native k8s sidecar container (cannot start/stop a sibling container from inside the pod) and client-of-existing-Deployment (no lifecycle control).
decisions.D-2: uar-driver = un-force now, delete later (user-confirmed). Drop the unconditional features = ["uar-driver"] edge at librefang-kernel/Cargo.toml:16.

build_vs_adopt: ZERO new third-party dependencies. ADOPT UAR's published image (COPY --from). REUSE librefang's own sidecar supervisor (sidecar.rs:502-520 already has spawn + restart + backoff) and its reqwest 0.13 (stream) / futures / tokio-stream stack for the HTTP+SSE client. BUILD only small in-tree pieces: generalize resolve_sidecar_command (sidecar.rs:728) from Telegram-only to stem-parameterized; add READY:{port} handshake + stdin-EOF shutdown; extend UarConfig; console controls; loud startup failure.
blocking_upstream_change: GQAdonis/universal-agent-runtime does NOT build/publish the `uar-sidecar` binary (grep -c uar-sidecar release.yml = 0; Dockerfile:225 builds only --bin universal-agent-runtime). Small additive change, same repo/branch family as sync-gqadonis-8c7377a1. BLOCKS G-1.
surrealdb_pin_finding: cargo tree -i surrealdb: universal-agent-runtime pins =3.2.1 (EXACT, rigid); surreal-memory pins 3.2.0 (CARET, flexible). UAR is the SOLE source of the lockstep pin — retiring the in-process link DOES free librefang to bump surrealdb unilaterally. Settles assessment open-question 3.
blockers_resolved_at_analyze: G-2 (no sidecar container in k8s) is N/A under D-1 — UAR runs as a child process inside the BossFang container, so no second container, Service, or NetworkPolicy change is needed.
